reasoning: The document has an excellent LLM fidelity score indicating strong overall content preservation, but automated detectors identified three major programmatic defects. These include retained header/footer elements, incorrect list conversion (roman numerals rendered as alphabetical), and incomplete or truncated image alt text. Due to the presence of these programmatic major issues, the document must be routed to HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Mana; Meeting Officer’s Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification; Meeting Officer’s Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 2 ordered list(s) rendered as alphabetical (A, B, C) but contain roman numeral markers (I, II, III) — e.g. "II. March 10, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary"; "II. Public Meeting Sign-in Forms"
concerns.analysis: roman_letter_confusion=2. The renderer misclassified roman numeral lists as alphabetical starting at letter 'I'. Item text retains original roman numerals alongside incorrect letter markers, breaking list semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 3 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (2 truncated mid-sentence, 1 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p1: "...arge letters "NC" where the 'N' is green and the '"; p1: "...k ink on a white background. It appears to read "S"; p1: "Unidentified image"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=3 (truncated=2, placeholder=1, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document heavily uses bold and underline formatting on the introductory phrases of list items (e.g., 'Dam Safety', 'Excavation', 'Groundwater Assessments'). This emphasis is completely lost in the output, which lacks both markdown for bold text and any indication of underline.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Dam Safety – There was a concern of a huge impact...
concerns.output_quote: Dam Safety – There was a concern of a huge impact...

action_items: Remove retained header/footer artifacts from the main content.
Correct the list marker types from alphabetical to roman numerals where applicable.
Update incomplete and truncated image alt text with meaningful descriptions.
Restore missing bold and underline visual emphasis on list item introductory phrases.
